options.label: Host with TCP Port
options.description: Connect with a hostname or IP address with the TCP port number

options.label: TNSNames Entry
options.description: Allows for specifying a tnsnames.ora entry for connection. Note: This is not supported in Grafana Cloud

fields.description: Connection-method discriminator: false selects 'Host with TCP Port' (host + port + database), true selects 'TNSNames Entry' (a tnsnames.ora entry). Written by the config editor's Connection methods selector.

fields.label: Host
fields.description: Hostname or IP Address with TCP port number.

fields.label: TNSName
fields.description: Use connection config specified in tnsnames.ora

options.label: Basic Authentication
options.description: Authenticate with an username and password

options.label: Kerberos Authentication
options.description: Kerberos authentication only works with TNSNames enabled, and must be configured with tnsnames.ora. Note: This is not supported in Grafana Cloud

fields.description: Authentication discriminator: false selects Basic Authentication (username + password), true selects Kerberos Authentication. The config editor only exposes Kerberos when useTNSNamesBasedConnection is true, though the backend also accepts Kerberos with a host + port connection.

fields.label: User
fields.description: An Oracle username with access to the specified database.

fields.label: Password
fields.description: An Oracle password with access to the specified database.

fields.label: Time zone
fields.description: Choose the default timezone of the Oracle server. Typically this is UTC.

fields.label: Connection Pool size
fields.description: Choose the maximum number of connections in the connection pool. Defaults to 50. Takes precedence over environment variable 'GF_PLUGINS_ORACLE_DATASOURCE_POOLSIZE' if set.

fields.label: Dataproxy Timeout
fields.description: Choose the maximum time in seconds to wait for a response. Defaults to 120 seconds. Takes precedence over environment variable 'GF_DATAPROXY_TIMEOUT' if set.

fields.label: Prefetch Row Size
fields.description: Row Prefetching allow you to set the number of rows to prefetch into the client while a result set is being populated during a query. This feature reduces the number of round trips to the server.

fields.label: Row Limit
fields.description: Maximum number of rows returned per query. Defaults to 1,000,000.

Parsed by the backend into DBConnectionOptions.DSTEnabled (pkg/models/settings.go:23) but never read when building the connection string. Declared in the frontend OracleOptions type (src/types.ts:9) but not written by the config editor — use_dst is a per-query/annotation option (src/types.ts:30, src/datasource.ts:109), not a datasource-level setting.

groups.title: Connection
groups.description: Provide information to connect to this datasource.

groups.title: Authentication
groups.description: Provide information to grant access to this datasource.

groups.title: Additional Settings
groups.description: Additional settings are optional settings that can be configured for more control over your data source.

relationships.description: TNSNames connection toggle and the tnsnames.ora entry it enables
Basic authentication credentials — supplied together when useKerberosAuthentication is false

instructions.msg: The Oracle datasource has two independent selectors, both stored in jsonData. Connection method: jsonData.useTNSNamesBasedConnection (false = 'Host with TCP Port', the default; true = 'TNSNames Entry'). Authentication: jsonData.useKerberosAuthentication (false = Basic Authentication with username + password, the default; true = Kerberos Authentication). The config editor only lets you pick Kerberos when useTNSNamesBasedConnection is true and it requires a configured tnsnames.ora; TNSNames and Kerberos are not supported in Grafana Cloud. The host+port is stored in the root-level 'url' field; database, user, tnsNamesEntry and the selectors live in jsonData; the password lives in secureJsonData.

Minimal payloads. Basic auth + Host with TCP Port: {"url":"<host>:1521","jsonData":{"database":"<db>","user":"<user>"},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<password>"}}. Basic auth + TNSNames: {"jsonData":{"useTNSNamesBasedConnection":true,"tnsNamesEntry":"<tnsnames.ora entry>","user":"<user>"},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<password>"}}. Kerberos + TNSNames (no user/password): {"jsonData":{"useTNSNamesBasedConnection":true,"useKerberosAuthentication":true,"tnsNamesEntry":"<tnsnames.ora entry>"}}.

secureJsonData.password is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a password is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (secureJsonFields.password == true) instead of secureJsonData. Kerberos authentication uses no username or password (they come from the Kerberos ticket / tnsnames.ora).

Legacy interpretation: in v3.3.0 the TNSNames entry was stored in the root-level 'url' field; v3.3.2 moved it to jsonData.tnsNamesEntry. Both the config editor and the backend migrate it: when useTNSNamesBasedConnection is true and jsonData.tnsNamesEntry is empty but root 'url' is set, the backend uses root 'url' as the tnsNamesEntry (pkg/models/settings.go:82-84). Do not assume an empty tnsNamesEntry means an unconfigured TNSNames datasource.

Connection string rules and pitfalls: the backend always connects in UTC and shifts times later, so jsonData.timezone_name (default 'UTC') only affects display, not the wire protocol (pkg/models/settings.go:110-115). For Host with TCP Port include the port in root 'url' (e.g. 'oracle.example.com:1521'); the backend builds '<user>/<password>@<url>/<database>?loc=<tz>'. Grant the Oracle user SELECT only on the specific tables Grafana should query — the backend does not sandbox statements, so any SQL (including DELETE/DROP) will run.

Optional performance knobs in jsonData, all applied by the backend when set: connectionPoolSize (default 50; env GF_PLUGINS_ORACLE_DATASOURCE_POOLSIZE overrides only when the stored value is 0), dataProxyTimeout in seconds (default 120; env GF_DATAPROXY_TIMEOUT overrides only when the stored value is 0), rowLimit (default 1000000; applied when the stored value is <= 0), and prefetchRowsCount (only added to the driver connection string when > 0). jsonData.use_dst is parsed by the backend but not used for connection building.
